questions.prompt: Hook rate is 45% but CTR is 0.4%. Diagnosis?

choices.text: Hook is strong — 45% is above the strong-baseline 40-50%. But the body isn't converting attention into clicks. CTR 0.4% on broad placements is well below the 1%+ healthy range. Pattern reads: the first 2-3 seconds stops scrolling but the body fails to make the user want to click. Fix the body (value prop clarity, social proof, CTA prominence); keep the hook.

questions.explanation: High hook + low CTR = hook stops scroll, body fails to convert attention. Fix the body.

questions.prompt: Account at 2x ROAS, hitting target. CPM rose 25% but CVR rose 40% over same period. Concerning?

choices.text: No — net positive. CPM in isolation isn't the problem; CPM vs CVR is. Here CVR grew faster than CPM, so revenue per impression actually improved. The auction got pricier (competition or saturation), but your creative + landing-page conversion improved more. Watch the trajectory — if CPM keeps rising while CVR plateaus, the picture flips. CPM matters relative to CVR change, not absolutely.

questions.explanation: CPM is meaningful only relative to CVR. Here CVR grew faster — net win.
